notes: Large-cap originator across oncology, vaccines, and internal medicine; broad portfolio, not a single-point chokepoint.
Pharma segment of a diversified healthcare conglomerate (also devices/consumer health, reported separately).
Also owns Genentech and a large diagnostics business; pharma division is one of several segments.
Spun off Sandoz (generics) as an independent company in 2023; now a pure originator.
Known outside North America as MSD; broad oncology and vaccine portfolio.
Dual-listed; strong oncology and rare disease pipeline.
Also a major vaccine maker (Sanofi Pasteur).
Currently capacity-constrained on GLP-1 drugs (tirzepatide), a demand-side not supply-chokepoint issue.
Spun off from Abbott in 2013; Humira/Skyrizi/Rinvoq-led immunology franchise.
Owns Novo Holdings' controlling stake structure; acquired Catalent's fill-finish sites in 2024-2025 to secure its own supply.

primary_segment: Generic drugs plus some branded specialty
notes: World's largest generics maker by volume; also holds branded specialty products (e.g. Austedo, Ajovy).

primary_segment: Generic drugs and active pharmaceutical ingredients
notes: India's largest pharma company by revenue; vertically integrated into API production.

primary_segment: Generic drugs and active pharmaceutical ingredients
notes: Major API and generic-finished-dose exporter to the US and EU.

primary_segment: Generic drugs and active pharmaceutical ingredients
notes: One of the largest suppliers of generic injectables and APIs to the US market.

primary_segment: Generic drugs and active pharmaceutical ingredients
notes: Major respiratory and generics maker; significant API self-supply.
Source of the 2018 global valsartan/sartan API recall (NDMA contamination); illustrates how concentrated a handful of Chinese/Indian API plants are for globally used generic drug classes.

primary_segment: Heparin active pharmaceutical ingredient (from porcine intestine)
notes: Central to the 2007-2008 contaminated-heparin crisis traced to a Changzhou, China sourcing/fractionation facility; heparin API supply remains concentrated in a small number of Chinese crude-heparin processors with no synthetic substitute at scale.

primary_segment: Biologics and sterile injectable contract manufacturing
notes: One of a handful of CDMOs with large-scale biologics/sterile-fill capacity that originators depend on; capacity bottlenecks here can delay launches industry-wide.

primary_segment: Sterile injectable fill-finish and softgel contract manufacturing
notes: Acquired by Novo Holdings in December 2024 (with select fill-finish sites sold on to Novo Nordisk); taken private, no longer separately traded.
Rapidly growing biologics CDMO capacity; a real alternative to WuXi Biologics amid Western derisking from Chinese biomanufacturing, which is why it is not (yet) itself flagged as a chokepoint.

primary_segment: Small-molecule drug substance, API, and R&D contract manufacturing
notes: Also dual-listed in Hong Kong (2359.HK); named alongside WuXi Biologics in the US BIOSECURE Act as a national-security supply-chain concern.

One of the world's largest biologics CDMOs; explicitly named in the proposed US BIOSECURE Act, which would restrict federal contracts with the firm, making it a geopolitical as well as an industrial chokepoint.
One of the 'big three' US drug wholesalers; McKesson, Cencora, and Cardinal Health together move roughly 90% of US pharmaceutical volume, so any one of the three carries systemic weight.
Second of the big-three US wholesalers; renamed from AmerisourceBergen in 2023.
Third of the big-three US wholesalers.

primary_segment: Pharmacy benefit management, health insurance (Aetna), and retail/specialty pharmacy
notes: CVS Caremark, Express Scripts (Cigna), and OptumRx (UnitedHealth) together administer roughly 80% of US prescription claims, giving the PBM layer outsized control over formulary access and rebates; CVS is the largest and also vertically integrated into retail dispensing.

primary_segment: Pharmacy benefit management and health insurance
notes: Owns Express Scripts, the second-largest US PBM.

primary_segment: Pharmacy benefit management and health insurance
notes: Owns OptumRx, the third of the big-three US PBMs.
